mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth accelerates corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0% signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending and equity valuations within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when UNRATE crosses below 6.0% in more than 50% of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The real interest rate, calculated as the difference between CPI inflation and the federal funds rate, dictates equity risk premiums. When real rates decline, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days increase.
failure_condition: Real rate changes show no correlation with 60-day SPX returns (p > 0.1) across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth expands corporate capacity utilization, leading to higher profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth decouples from SPX returns during supply chain disruptions or inventory gluts.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1000 thousand units indicate robust construction activity and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are flat or negative when HOUST crosses above 1000 thousand units in the majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll gains fail to translate into positive 60-day SPX returns when unemployment remains sticky above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns remain positive despite consecutive Fed fund rate hikes over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines more than 5 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive despite retail sales falling by more than 5 percent in the triggering month.
